product.name: ArWoWear T2 Flame Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T2 Flame Pro is light weight 260 g/m², 100% cotton, vat dyed, flame retardant workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 and EN ISO 11611 certification.
It is durable up to 100 washing.
100% Cotton Twill, Flame Retardant Fabric, 260 gr/m'2
260 g/m², 100% Cotton, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>800N; Weft>600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash resistance directly indicate welding/metalworking application.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit electric-arc protection rating (EN 61482, ATPV) directly supports high-voltage/electrical hazard protection use in the energy sector.
EN ISO 11612 flame and heat protection is present but this standard alone covers general industrial FR workwear, not explicit firefighter turnout gear; no firefighting-specific wording is present.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service wording; only generic FR coverall/workwear application is stated.

primary.rationale: Explicit EN ISO 11611 welding protection certification and molten-metal splash resistance point directly to metalworking/welding as a dominant intended use, alongside general FR workwear positioning.

secondary.rationale: Explicit electric-arc protection rating (EN 61482 with ATPV value) independently supports use in electrical/energy sector hazard protection, beyond generic FR workwear.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: General FR workwear framing exists but narrower welding and energy hazard certifications dominate the product's clearest stated use.
EN ISO 11612 flame/heat protection is present but this is general industrial FR standard, not explicit firefighter turnout gear wording.
No explicit military, police, or uniformed-service wording present.
